violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of orange residue and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023

Octopus and ROP fish was observed thawing on the counter and in packages still intact. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Octopus relocated to refrigerator and ROP fish packages were cut into. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023
The (sushi) handsink is lacking hot water. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023
The plumbing to the (sushi) handsink is in poor repair. Leak observed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2023

The walls in the ice machine area are constructed of sheetrock. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the 3 bay sink area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2023
